profile.name: Krishnendu Goswami
profile.title: Luminar — Photography

awards.name: Wildlife Photographer of the Year — Highly Commended
Nature TTL Photographer of the Year — Gold Award

profile.bioExcerpt: Krishnendu Goswami is a nationally exhibited photographer whose work navigates the quiet intersection of the natural world and human presence.
profile.philosophy: I don't chase photographs. I wait for the world to reveal itself — one breath at a time. The camera is simply an extension of that patience.
bioParagraphs: Based in India, their journey with the camera began over two decades ago. What started as a personal quest to document vanishing landscapes became a lifelong dedication to the ethics and art of visual storytelling. Their images have appeared in natural history magazines, and have been exhibited across galleries internationally.

Every expedition is a partnership with time and light. By spending weeks in remote areas—from the high altitudes of the Zanskar range to the dense jungles of the Western Ghats—they seek to capture not just a scene, but the silent atmosphere and emotional weight of a place. Their approach is marked by absolute respect for habitats, always prioritizing conservation ethics over capturing a shot.

stories.desc: A four-year winter fieldwork series documenting the fragile high-altitude movement corridors of the ghost of the mountains in Spiti and Ladakh.
stories.title: The Last Snow Leopard Corridors

stories.desc: A visual study of the Gangotri glacier's retreat and the changing riparian communities along the first hundred kilometres of the river path.
stories.title: Ganga: The Receding Source

journalEntries.title: Night Photography Without GPS: Navigating Dark Skies the Old Way
journalEntries.excerpt: Star charts, local guides, and the irreplaceable value of arriving the night before — a guide to planning astrophotography expeditions.

journalEntries.title: rajanigandha

journalEntries.contentHtml:
check if your blog editor supports "Raw HTML" blocks or "Trusted Content" rendering.

1. Text Emphasis & Markup Test

  • This is bold text for strong emphasis.

  • This is italicized text for subtle emphasis.

  • This is bold and italicized for maximum emphasis.

  • This is strikethrough text to indicate obsolete information.

  • This is inline code for highlighting specific terminology.

2. Mathematical & Scientific Notation

When writing about the physics of light or orbital mechanics, your blog must render mathematical symbols correctly:

  • The Exposure Equation: The light collected ($L$) can be represented as $L = A \cdot t \cdot I$, where $A$ is the aperture area, $t$ is time, and $I$ is intensity.

  • Kepler’s Third Law: $P^2 = a^3$, where $P$ is the orbital period and $a$ is the semi-major axis.

  • Subscripts & Superscripts: $H_2O$ and $E=mc^2$.

  • Greek Symbols: The wavelength of light is often denoted by $\lambda$, while the pixel scale is $\theta$.

3. Special Characters & Symbols

Ensure your character encoding (UTF-8) supports these common scientific and decorative symbols:

  • Mathematical: $\pm$, $\approx$, $\neq$, $\geq$, $\leq$, $\int$, $\sum$, $\infty$

  • Astronomy: ☉ (Sun), ☽ (Moon), ♃ (Jupiter), ♄ (Saturn)

  • General/Typography: ©, ®, ™, §, †, ‡, ¤, ¶, •, ‣

4. Hierarchical Elements

Blockquote Note: This section is for highlighting important warnings, such as: "Always ensure your tripod is weighted down to prevent micro-vibrations during long-exposure imaging."

Procedural List (Ordered)

  1. Alignment: Calibrate the equatorial mount.

  2. Focusing: Achieve critical focus using a Bahtinov mask.

  3. Capturing: Execute the sequence of light frames, dark frames, and bias frames.

Comparative Data Layout

Feature

Importance

Impact on Final Image

Aperture

High

Affects diffraction spikes

ISO Speed

Medium

Affects signal-to-noise ratio

Shutter

High

Determines star trailing

System check complete. If all elements above (especially the equations and symbols) appear with correct spacing and font rendering, your blog’s infrastructure is successfully configured for technical content.

Do you need help testing any specific CSS overrides for these elements, such as custom font weights or color coding for the equations?

journalEntries.contentHtml:
1. Text Emphasis & Markup Test

  • This is bold text for strong emphasis.

  • This is italicized text for subtle emphasis.

  • This is bold and italicized for maximum emphasis.

  • This is strikethrough text to indicate obsolete information.

  • This is inline code for highlighting specific terminology.

2. Mathematical & Scientific Notation

When writing about the physics of light or orbital mechanics, your blog must render mathematical symbols correctly:

  • The Exposure Equation: The light collected ($L$) can be represented as $L = A \cdot t \cdot I$, where $A$ is the aperture area, $t$ is time, and $I$ is intensity.

  • Kepler’s Third Law: $P^2 = a^3$, where $P$ is the orbital period and $a$ is the semi-major axis.

  • Subscripts & Superscripts: $H_2O$ and $E=mc^2$.

  • Greek Symbols: The wavelength of light is often denoted by $\lambda$, while the pixel scale is $\theta$.

3. Special Characters & Symbols

Ensure your character encoding (UTF-8) supports these common scientific and decorative symbols:

  • Mathematical: $\pm$, $\approx$, $\neq$, $\geq$, $\leq$, $\int$, $\sum$, $\infty$

  • Astronomy: ☉ (Sun), ☽ (Moon), ♃ (Jupiter), ♄ (Saturn)

  • General/Typography: ©, ®, ™, §, †, ‡, ¤, ¶, •, ‣

4. Hierarchical Elements

Blockquote Note: This section is for highlighting important warnings, such as: "Always ensure your tripod is weighted down to prevent micro-vibrations during long-exposure imaging."

Procedural List (Ordered)

  1. Alignment: Calibrate the equatorial mount.

  2. Focusing: Achieve critical focus using a Bahtinov mask.

  3. Capturing: Execute the sequence of light frames, dark frames, and bias frames.

Comparative Data Layout

Feature

Importance

Impact on Final Image

Aperture

High

Affects diffraction spikes

ISO Speed

Medium

Affects signal-to-noise ratio

Shutter

High

Determines star trailing

System check complete. If all elements above (especially the equations and symbols) appear with correct spacing and font rendering, your blog’s infrastructure is successfully configured for technical content.

Do you need help testing any specific CSS overrides for these elements, such as custom font weights or color coding for the equations?

macro.desc: The perspective we often overlook, looked over at a different scale.
macro.title: Ma

nature.desc: Life unfiltered, away from human design — capturing the intricate textures of organic existence and the subtle patterns woven by earth, water, and time.
nature.title: Nat

photos.story: The day’s very first sunlight pierces through the forest canopy, casting a dramatic, golden beam that crowns a freshly bloomed cluster of pink wildflowers. The stark, high-contrast interplay between the radiant, backlit petals and the deep, moody shadows of the background perfectly embodies the raw, quiet energy of a new day beginning.
photos.title: GENESIS
photos.caption: The beginning

splits.desc: A cinematic visual narrative of isolated frames, binding distinct dimensions of time and perspective into a singular, interconnected canvas.
splits.title: Split

photos.story: A three-panel photographic study capturing the atmosphere of the Rash Utsav rituals. The triptych juxtaposes moments of quiet devotion and hands raised toward a sacred flame against the sharp silhouette of a ceremonial trident. Swirling incense smoke, rich green undertones, and the warm reflection of brass artifacts document the vivid energy of the heritage festival.
photos.title: রাশের Rush
photos.caption: fathom of faith

street.desc: The chaotic harmony of public spaces — capturing the geometry of environments shaped by habit, and the fleeting micro-dramas of a single frame.
street.title: Str

photos.loc: New Digha Sea Beach , West Bengal - India
photos.story: A study in identity, symmetry, and social geometry. Captured against a brooding, monochromatic horizon, two classic masks dominate the foreground while nine distant figures divide into three distinct groups along the sea wall. This visual rhythm creates a subtle, unsettling dialogue between the overt anonymity of plastic faces and the quiet, invisible facades worn within human connections. It leaves the viewer with a lingering question: where is the third mask? Is it the crowd watching the horizon, or is it hiding within us?
photos.title: 3 MASK-ETTERS
photos.caption: The masks within

travel.desc: Journeys documented with curiosity — cultures, borders, and the roads that connect them.
travel.title: Tra

portrait.desc: Faces that hold entire histories — quiet collaborations between photographer and subject.
portrait.title: Por

wildlife.desc: Intimate encounters with creatures in their natural habitat — patience rendered visible, silence translated into light.
wildlife.title: Wild

photos.loc: Padmaja Naidu Himalayan Zoological Park Darjeeling, West Bengal - India
photos.story: Though the common leopard could be confused with the jaguar, the jaguar is less stocky and have its rosette patterned marking on its coat with no internal spots. DISTRIBUTION : Indian subspecies of common leopard is widely distributed in India, extends into Nepal, Myanmar and Sri Lanka. In Himalayas found upto an altitude of 3000 mts. FOOD : Leopards will kill and eat anything they can over power easily. Breeding : Breed all the year round. Has a gestation period of 87~94 days and gives birth to 1~2 cubs per litter. Common Leopard has been in Darjeeling Zoo since its inception and have done well in captivity.
photos.title: COMMON LEOPARD
photos.caption: Panthera pardus

photos.loc: Padmaja Naidu Himalayan Zoological Park, Darjeeling, West Bengal - India
photos.story: Red Pandas are medium sized bear like mammal with a thick rusty to deep chestnut colored coat. The muzzle, eye patches and the fronts of the large pointed ears are white along with broad white cheek patches. The tail is long, bushy and with ginger rings. Red pandas are classified as Endangered on the IUCN Red List, with their wild population estimated to have dropped by 50% over the last three generations. Fewer than 10,000 individuals are left in the wild today.main reasons include: Habitat Loss: Deforestation from logging and agriculture shrinks their Himalayan forest homes. Dietary Risk: They depend on bamboo for 98% of their diet, making them highly vulnerable when forests vanish. Poaching & Traps: They face illegal hunting for their fur and are often caught in traps meant for other wildlife. DISTRIBUTION : Central Nepal to Arunachal Pradesh and this stretch includes Sikkim and Darjeeling Himalayas. The distribution is further extended from Arunachal Pradesh to Myanmar and China. FOOD : 90% of Red Panda's diet consists of bamboo leaves and the rest consist of bamboo shoots, fruits and berries. Breeding : Mating season is between January to March. Gestation period is 100-150 days to deliver 1-4 cubs in a litter during May / June. Survives and breed well in captivity.
photos.title: RED PANDA
photos.caption: Ailurus fulgens fulgens

landscape.desc: The earth at its most eloquent — mountains, valleys, and the horizons between them, captured in moments of transient light.
landscape.title: Land

astrophotography.desc: Intercepting photons at the terminal end of their journey through the cosmic vacuum, translating different eras of space-time into a synchronized grid of pixels.
astrophotography.title: Astro

photos.story: The Target: I have captured NGC 7000, famously known as the North America Nebula, located in the constellation Cygnus. The Emission Glow: The deep pinkish-red hues come from massive clouds of hydrogen gas (Hα or Hydrogen-alpha emission). This gas is being ionized and energized by the intense ultraviolet radiation from hot, massive stars hidden within or near the nebula.The Dark Nebulae: The striking dark structures cutting into the bright red areas are cosmic dust lanes (primarily LDN 935). This dense interstellar dust blocks the light from the stars and glowing gas behind it, creating the distinct silhouette that shapes the "Gulf of Mexico" and "Hudson Bay" features of the nebula. The Cygnus Wall: The brightest, most defined vertical structure near the center is the Cygnus Wall. This is a region of rapid, concentrated star formation where young stars are sculpting the surrounding gas with powerful stellar winds.
photos.title: The Cygnus Arm
photos.caption: milky way band
